name: Grade 10 Mathematics — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 10 pathway for probability, statistics, algebraic proof, and data-based decisions. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 10 knowledge and transferable skills in probability, statistics, algebraic proof, and data-based decisions.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: probability, statistics, algebraic proof, and data-based decisions
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about probability, statistics, algebraic proof, and data-based decisions and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about probability, statistics, algebraic proof, and data-based decisions, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.

successCriteria: The learner completes a grade 10 task connected to probability, statistics, algebraic proof, and data-based decisions.
